channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Argentina's Milei doubles down on Trump bromance as the world reels from trade shock
item.description: While the world’s leaders were wringing their hands over President Donald Trump’s announcement of sweeping tariffs on U.S. imports, Argentina’s right-wing president was ebullient, feted at Trump’s Mar-a-Lago club.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Debre, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How Trump's latest tariffs could affect your wallet
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump has unveiled his latest tariffs, and they could have significant implications for your wallet. Trump's sweeping new tariffs, on top of previous levies and retaliation worldwide, are expected to increase prices for everyday items.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and CORA LEWIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: England’s Wrestling Church seeks converts with baptisms and body slams
item.description: SHIPLEY, England (AP) — Sitting around a wrestling ring, churchgoers roared as local hero Billy O’Keeffe body-slammed a fighter named Disciple. Beneath stained-glass windows, they whooped and cheered as burly, tattooed wresters tumbled into the aisle during a six-man tag-team battle.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Officials say a Russian strike in central Ukraine kills 14 people, including six children
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian ballistic missile strike Friday on a central Ukrainian city killed at least 14 people, including six children, Ukrainian officials said, as U.S. and European leaders pressed Russia to accept a ceasefire in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Panamanian ex-leader Martinelli remains in the Nicaraguan Embassy after window to leave expired
item.description: Panama’s ex-President Ricardo Martinelli remains inside the Nicaraguan Embassy more than a year after he first entered and a week after Panama said it would allow him to leave.
item.dc:creator: Alma Solís, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's tariffs hit uninhabited islands filled with penguins
item.description: The implementation of tariffs on the islands did not go unnoticed by Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: South Koreans are still puzzling over why their leader declared martial law
item.description: SEOUL (AP) — Dancing and consoling hugs. Wild whoops and anguished screams. Tears, both of joy and rage.
item.dc:creator: FOSTER KLUG and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: As stock market continues plummeting over tariffs, Trump spends the day at his golf course
item.description: President Donald Trump says his trade policies will never change despite sending the global economy into a tailspin by announcing tariffs on foreign imports.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein And Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Houthis shoot down 3rd US reaper drone as Trump administration continues daily airstrikes
item.description: The Houthis shot down another U.S. Reaper drone, the third one that has been destroyed by the terror organization since early March.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kinzinger posts 'Step Brothers' clip to mock Trump tariffs
item.description: Former Rep. Adam Kinzinger (R-Ill.) posted a clip from the popular comedy "Step Brothers" to mock President Trump, Republicans and Fox News over the latest tariffs on foreign nations that were announced this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Kurdish fighters leave northern city in Syria as part of deal with central government
item.description: Scores of U.S.-backed Kurdish fighters have left two neighborhoods in Syria’s northern city of Aleppo part of a deal with the central government in Damascus.
item.dc:creator: Ghaith Alsayed, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says China 'panicked' with tariff response
item.description: President Trump on Friday chastised China after Beijing responded to major U.S. tariffs with equal duties of its own on U.S. goods. "China played it wrong, they panicked - the one thing they cannot afford to do!" Trump posted on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Danish leader tells the US 'you cannot annex another country' as she visits Greenland
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — Denmark's prime minister has told the U.S. during a visit to Greenland that “you cannot annex another country,” even with the argument that international security is at stake. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China imposing 34 percent tariffs on all US goods
item.description: China, in the wake of President Trump's latest tariffs on foreign trading partners, announced Friday it will hit the U.S. with a 34 percent reciprocal tax on imports starting next week.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Israeli strike on a school in Gaza kills at least 27 people, Palestinian health officials say
item.description: DEIR AL BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes killed at least 100 Palestinians across the Gaza Strip on Thursday, including 27 or more sheltering at a school, according to Palestinian medical authorities, in a stepped-up offensive that Israel’s military said is intended to pressure Hamas and e
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What to expect in South Korea now that Yoon has been removed from office
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The South Korean Constitutional Court’s decision to formally unseat President Yoon Suk Yeol is another test for the country’s democracy after the conservative-liberal divide deepened over his imposition of martial law and subsequent impeachment.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Haiti taking more steps against gangs after thousands of protesters demand security
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — The leader of Haiti’s transitional presidential council tried to allay surging alarm among Haitians over rampant gang violence by acknowledging Thursday that the country has “become hell for everyone” and promising new measures to stop the bloodshed.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump sued over China tariffs
item.description: President Trump was sued Thursday over the 20 percent tariffs he imposed on Chinese goods in the weeks leading up to Wednesday’s broader announcement. It marks the first known legal challenge against Trump’s tariffs, which have fulfilled a campaign promise and rattled financial markets.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: Boasberg floats contempt hearings on deportation flights, suspects 'bad faith' actions from Trump admin
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge James Boasberg hinted at possible contempt proceedings as he grilled a government lawyer Thursday about whether the Trump administration violated his court order by failing to turn around planes carrying migrants to a Salvadoran prison.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Myanmar earthquake death toll rises to 3,145 as more bodies found
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from the earthquake that hit Myanmar nearly a week ago rose Thursday to 3,145 as search and rescue teams found more bodies, the military-led government said, and humanitarian aid groups scrambled to provide survivors medical care and shelter.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yoon Suk Yeol removed as South Korea's president over short-lived martial law
item.description: South Korea’s Constitutional Court has unanimously removed Yoon Suk Yeol as president after he threw the nation into turmoil by declaring martial law and sending troops to parliament in an ill-fated effort to break through legislative gridlock.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim And Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump urged to review UN immunity, lax visa rules amid national security concerns
item.description: As the U.S. weighs U.N. funding cuts, experts urge the Trump administration to review the world body's visa process and staff immunity over continued security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada announces 25 percent tariff on non-USMCA compliant US auto imports
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney announced Thursday his country will impose 25 percent tariffs on U.S. auto imports that do not comply with the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) on free trade.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Rubio tries to reassure wary allies of US commitment to NATO as Trump sends mixed signals
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio and the Trump administration’s new envoy to NATO are seeking to reassure wary members of the U.S. commitment to the alliance. Rubio on Thursday decried “hysteria and hyperbole” in the media about U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Slovaks rally to protest against legislation that critics say is harmful to NGOs
item.description: BRATISLAVA, Slovakia (AP) — Thousands marched through Slovakia's capital on Thursday before a planned rally in front of parliament to condemn draft legislation that critics consider detrimental for many nongovernmental organizations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Post-earthquake ceasefires in Myanmar offer rare opportunity for disaster diplomacy
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Temporary ceasefires announced by warring groups in Myanmar in the wake of last week’s devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake represent a rare de-escalation of a conflict that since 2021 has taken thousands of lives and uprooted more than 3 million people.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Burma earthquake death toll rises to 3,085, humanitarian crisis deepens
item.description: More than 3,000 people have died from the 7.7 magnitude earthquake that struck Burma last week, which has sent the country into a deeper humanitarian crisis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio arrives in Brussels for NATO talks amid unease over Trump’s agenda
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived at in Brussels Thursday for talks that allies hope will shed light on the U.S.’ future plans in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ontario premier ‘cautiously optimistic’ amid Trump tariffs
item.description: “Make no mistake about it, Canadians love Americans. They love the U.S., and I do, too,” Doug Ford said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tariffs likely to spur recession: Moody's economist
item.description: Mark Zandi said the situation may not be as grave if countries do not respond “in kind” to the president’s tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Canada's PM Carney vows to ‘fight’ Trump’s tariffs, other world leaders weigh impact
item.description: President Trump's newest tariffs sparked pledges by Canada's prime minster to 'fight' back while some said they were committed to working with partners and others look to take on Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel will establish a new security corridor across Gaza to pressure Hamas
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Wednesday that Israel is establishing a new security corridor across the Gaza Strip to pressure Hamas, suggesting it would cut off the southern city of Rafah, which Israel has ordered evacuated, from the rest of the Palestinian
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and ISAAC SCHARF, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli leader Netanyahu will visit Hungary, defying an international arrest warrant
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is scheduled to arrive in Hungary on Wednesday to meet with its nationalist prime minister despite an international arrest warrant for the Israeli leader over the war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Aftershock from a political 'earthquake' as Le Pen barred from presidential run in 2027
item.description: Le Pen called her ban from running a "nuclear bomb" by the system, after a court convicted her of embezzlement and blocked her from office for five years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel prepares to move deeper into Gaza and orders Rafah evacuated
item.description: The Israeli military indicated on Monday it could soon launch another major offensive in Rafah and ordered most of the southernmost city in the Gaza Strip evacuated. Israel ended its ceasefire with Hamas and renewed its air and ground war earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chair of African charity Prince Harry co-founded says the royal orchestrated a bullying campaign
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The chairperson of an African charity co-founded by Prince Harry accused the royal on Sunday of orchestrating a bullying and harassment campaign to try to force her out as she pushed back following his abrupt resignation from the organization.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600 and leaves countless buried
item.description: MANDALAY, Myanmar (AP) — The smell of decaying bodies permeated the streets of Myanmar's second-largest city on Sunday as people worked frantically by hand to clear rubble in the hope of finding someone still alive, two days after a massive earthquake struck that killed more than 1,600 people and le
item.dc:creator: THEIN ZAW, DAVID RISING and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, WGN 9
